```json
{
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "Clean-enough layering with disciplined policy isolation, but stops short of level 4 because services call raw SQL directly and the frontend is organized by technical role rather than by feature.",
  "evidence": {
    "layers_present": "backend-tree.txt shows policy/, services/, routes/ as distinct directories plus db.ts, errors.ts, types.ts, validators.ts at the top level — clear routes → services → policy/db direction.",
    "policy_centralized": "policy/index.ts holds MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY, LATE_FINE_CAP_CENTS, RESERVATION_NOTIFICATION_HOURS, FINE_BORROW_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_CENTS plus pure functions canBorrow / computeFineCents / nextReservationToNotify; loan-service.ts imports them rather than re-declaring (line 4).",
    "named_error_hierarchy": "loan-handler.ts catches DomainError and routes to err.statusCode; loan-service.ts throws ConflictError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, PaymentRequiredError from ../errors.js — a small named hierarchy used at both layers.",
    "http_layer_thin": "routes/loans.ts (loan-handler.ts) is 46 lines: validates with zod, calls loanService, translates DomainError to HTTP — no SQL or business rules inline.",
    "no_cycles_no_orphans": "madge.json: {\"circular\": [], \"orphans\": []}.",
    "file_sizes_healthy": "Largest non-test source file is MemberProfilePage.tsx at 251 lines; CheckoutPage.tsx 237; loanService.ts 153; api.ts 153; db.ts 130. Only file >300 lines is services/index.test.ts (493) which is a test, not production code. No god-files.",
    "frontend_split": "frontend-tree.txt has pages/, components/, api.ts, context.tsx — a clear pages/components/api split; routes file imports BooksPage, BookDetailPage, etc. cleanly.",
    "level_4_gaps": [
      "loan-service.ts calls db.prepare(...) directly throughout (e.g., lines 13, 16, 30, 34, 50, 78) — no thin typed DB-access abstraction, so services aren't unit-testable in isolation.",
      "Frontend organizes by technical role (pages/, components/) rather than by feature — there is no checkout/, members/, books/ owning their own components+hooks+API calls. Modal/CreateBookModal/CreateMemberModal sit in a flat components/ directory.",
      "Reservation state-machine transitions are not contained in one module: loanService.borrowBook flips a reservation 'notified' → 'fulfilled' (lines 89–93) and loanService.returnBook flips 'waiting' → 'notified' (lines 142–144), in addition to whatever lives in reservationService.ts. The state machine is split across services rather than owned by a single named module (level-5 requirement)."
    ],
    "level_5_gaps": "Beyond the level-4 gaps above, services skip an abstraction and reach straight into the DB driver, so the dependency graph is not strictly inward toward policy in a layered sense — routes go through services, but services blend SQL + rules instead of going through a typed query module."
  }
}
```

parsed.rationale: Clean-enough layering with disciplined policy isolation, but stops short of level 4 because services call raw SQL directly and the frontend is organized by technical role rather than by feature.

evidence.layers_present: backend-tree.txt shows policy/, services/, routes/ as distinct directories plus db.ts, errors.ts, types.ts, validators.ts at the top level — clear routes → services → policy/db direction.
evidence.policy_centralized: policy/index.ts holds MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS, LOAN_PERIOD_DAYS, LATE_FINE_RATE_CENTS_PER_DAY, LATE_FINE_CAP_CENTS, RESERVATION_NOTIFICATION_HOURS, FINE_BORROW_BLOCK_THRESHOLD_CENTS plus pure functions canBorrow / computeFineCents / nextReservationToNotify; loan-service.ts imports them rather than re-declaring (line 4).
evidence.named_error_hierarchy: loan-handler.ts catches DomainError and routes to err.statusCode; loan-service.ts throws ConflictError, ForbiddenError, NotFoundError, PaymentRequiredError from ../errors.js — a small named hierarchy used at both layers.
evidence.http_layer_thin: routes/loans.ts (loan-handler.ts) is 46 lines: validates with zod, calls loanService, translates DomainError to HTTP — no SQL or business rules inline.
evidence.file_sizes_healthy: Largest non-test source file is MemberProfilePage.tsx at 251 lines; CheckoutPage.tsx 237; loanService.ts 153; api.ts 153; db.ts 130. Only file >300 lines is services/index.test.ts (493) which is a test, not production code. No god-files.
evidence.frontend_split: frontend-tree.txt has pages/, components/, api.ts, context.tsx — a clear pages/components/api split; routes file imports BooksPage, BookDetailPage, etc. cleanly.
level_4_gaps: loan-service.ts calls db.prepare(...) directly throughout (e.g., lines 13, 16, 30, 34, 50, 78) — no thin typed DB-access abstraction, so services aren't unit-testable in isolation.

Frontend organizes by technical role (pages/, components/) rather than by feature — there is no checkout/, members/, books/ owning their own components+hooks+API calls. Modal/CreateBookModal/CreateMemberModal sit in a flat components/ directory.

Reservation state-machine transitions are not contained in one module: loanService.borrowBook flips a reservation 'notified' → 'fulfilled' (lines 89–93) and loanService.returnBook flips 'waiting' → 'notified' (lines 142–144), in addition to whatever lives in reservationService.ts. The state machine is split across services rather than owned by a single named module (level-5 requirement).
evidence.level_5_gaps: Beyond the level-4 gaps above, services skip an abstraction and reach straight into the DB driver, so the dependency graph is not strictly inward toward policy in a layered sense — routes go through services, but services blend SQL + rules instead of going through a typed query module.
